title_001: Burnout Traffic Light — What color is your signal right now?
html_003: Burnout doesn’t hit you out of nowhere —
html_004: it comes on like a traffic light:
html_007: What color is your signal right now?
html_012: Every answer lights up the signal at the top in real time.
html_013: There are no right answers — just be honest with yourself right now.
html_021: , how often has this been true for you?
js_002: What you need most right now is real, purposeless rest. Give yourself permission to do nothing at all.
js_004: A closed-off heart is just armor to protect you. Start rebuilding with one safe person, a little at a time.
js_006: Instead of big wins, count one tiny “I did it” every day. That’s how your sense of efficacy comes back.
js_008: Burnout hits the body first. The fastest fix is the basics — sleep, water, meals — in that order.
js_009: The moment I wake up, I already think “here goes another day” and feel drained.
js_010: By the end of the day I feel emotionally empty and don’t want to do anything.
js_011: Even after resting I don’t feel rested — the tiredness just keeps piling up.
js_012: Dealing with people feels more and more like a chore, so I just go through the motions.
js_013: Things that used to matter now feel like “whatever, who cares.”
js_014: I’ve shut the door on my feelings and I’m just doing the bare minimum to get by.
js_015: What I do feels like it doesn’t really matter.
js_016: Even when I finish something, there’s no pride — just relief that it’s “finally over.”
js_017: I can’t focus like I used to, and I slip up or forget things more often.
js_018: Body signals like headaches, an upset stomach, or stiff shoulders show up more often.
js_019: I struggle to fall asleep, or I wake up still not feeling refreshed.
js_020: I’m barely getting through the day on coffee, sugar, late-night snacks, or a drink.
js_027: Your energy is flowing right now. The signal is green — you’re running smoothly, no major issues.
js_028: Keep the good routines you’ve got — sleep, healthy distance, small joys. With burnout, prevention is the easiest cure.
js_029: Push too hard just because you’re green, and yellow shows up in no time. Once a week, ask yourself: “How am I really doing lately?”
js_032: Your body and mind are signaling “slow down.” It’s not too late — but now is the time to adjust.
js_033: Delete one thing you can drop this week. Then put recovery time on your calendar — not as a “to-do,” but as an appointment.
js_034: Wave off yellow as “I’m still fine” and it turns red. Start by tending to whichever area is most drained.
js_037: You’ve been holding on for a long time. This isn’t weak willpower — it’s your body’s signal that you’ve run too long.
js_038: Stop whatever you can stop right now. And don’t carry it alone — put your current state into words to someone you trust, or a professional.
js_039: Red isn’t the stage for enduring more — it’s the stage for getting help. If sleep, appetite, or mood have been hard for over two weeks, please talk to a professional.
js_044: 🟢 You’re in a healthy green right now!
js_045: 🟡 Just noticing is already half the recovery
js_046: 🔴 You’ve made it this far — that took real strength
jst_001: Cumulative ratio based on the questions answered so far
jst_002: The area sending the strongest signal is
jst_003: . You’re still green, but keep a gentle eye on just this area and you can stay healthy for the long run.
jst_014: This isn’t a medical diagnosis — it’s a self-check to help you reflect.
jst_015: weeks or more of feeling this low, please reach out to a professional. Mental-health helpline
jst_018: What color is your signal? Check yours too

jst_020: A quiet, caring effect when red

_app.title: Burnout Traffic Light
_app.desc: What color is your signal right now? A 12-question self-check for burnout.
